Name: training_free_geometric_diagnostics
Title: Geometric Probes for Training-Free Performance Estimation in Machine Learning Models
Short Hypothesis: This research proposes that a set of geometric probes can be developed to provide training-free diagnostics of machine learning models’ performance, offering insights into model selection and hyperparameter tuning without requiring extensive training or computational resources.
Related Work: Existing work such as NEAR focuses on pre-estimation of model performance but does not employ a geometric approach to understand the underlying structural properties of representations. This proposal distinguishes itself by integrating geometric diagnostics—such as curvature analysis and Hessian conditioning—into the model evaluation process, thereby leveraging mathematical frameworks that are often overlooked in performance estimations.
Abstract: The rapid evolution of machine learning (ML) necessitates robust methods for model evaluation and selection, particularly under constraints of computational resources. In this proposal, we introduce an innovative approach that employs geometric probes for training-free diagnostics of model performance. By leveraging subspace diagnostics derived from curvature-aware metrics and spectral quantization theory, we aim to create a framework that quantitatively assesses the stability and generalization potential of ML models without the need for training. These geometric probes will provide insights into gradient covariance decay and feature entanglement, enabling researchers to make informed decisions regarding model architecture and hyperparameters. We hypothesize that such an approach can yield significant improvements in empirical performance and efficiency, particularly in resource-constrained environments. Our framework will be validated through systematic ablation studies and multi-seed variance quantification, ensuring rigor and reproducibility in results.
Experiments: 1. Model Selection: Compare the proposed geometric probes with traditional performance metrics across a variety of ML models (e.g., CNNs, RNNs) on benchmark datasets (e.g., MNIST, CIFAR-10). - Metric: Accuracy, F1-score, and computational overhead. 2. Hyperparameter Tuning: Utilize geometric diagnostics to optimize hyperparameters (e.g., learning rate, batch size) without training the models iteratively. - Metric: Performance improvement compared to standard tuning methods. 3. Robustness Testing: Assess the sensitivity of models to perturbations using geometric properties, analyzing how well the probes predict model robustness. - Metric: Stability radius and performance variance under noise.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Data Dependency: The quality of the geometric probes may depend on the dataset's complexity and the inherent structure of the data. Theoretical Assumptions: The approach relies on assumptions about the geometric properties of representations, which may not hold universally across all models. Computational Constraints: While the proposal emphasizes training-free evaluation, some initial computations may still be required to establish the geometric metrics.
